title: hieracium carolinianum subsp. rugelii
intro: Hieracium carolinianum subsp. rugelii is a subspecies of the Hawkweed plant, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating kidney and urinary issues, due to its diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating joint pain, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations for Hieracium carolinianum subsp. rugelii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although its efficacy and safety are not extensively scientifically proven.
